actionable_feedback: Ensure the workbook can be machine-parsed by graders: include an exported machine-readable representation of formulas (CSV of formulas or a worksheet containing formulas as text) and/or provide a reproducible script (e.g., Python notebook) that computes the same intermediate fields so automated graders can verify cell-level calculations without openpyxl.

Embed an explicit, canonical scoring equation and derivation in both the memo and the workbook (include the exact cell references used to calculate observed_risk_score and margin). If multiple reasonable interpretations exist, enumerate them and show results for each, labeling the preferred production equation.

Add a machine-readable final_decision_table (CSV or JSON) as a deliverable artifact that exactly matches the workbook/memo results (record_id, observed_risk_score, margin, classification, sensitivity flags, data_needs_review, final_needs_review).

Materially reconcile R3 and R4: document the source of expected_damage_usd values, reconcile differences with property_value_m * damage_ratio, and record the outcome in the memo and workbook (update reconciliation_status and include supporting citations).

Explicitly show workbook provenance: a worksheet or sheet-level notes listing row-level citations back to input/task.json and a short checklist confirming no external links. If true, call this out and include screenshots or a text export of the citation cells if the grader cannot parse XLSX.
For auditability, add a 'FORMULAS' sheet containing textual formula strings and a 'CALC_TRACE' sheet mapping intermediate field names to the exact formula/cell ranges used.

Update the memo to call out which deliverable files contain the machine-readable exports and to provide brief instructions for a manual verifier on where to inspect formula lineage if automated parsing fails.
artifact_specific_comments: attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] Grader preview indicates parse failure (openpyxl_unavailable). The memo asserts visible formula lineage and row-level citations but these claims require manual verification or a formula export so automated graders can confirm.

attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] Memo is clear and contains required sections (scope/inputs, scoring assumption flagged as needs_review, final decision table, sensitivity, remediation analysis, recommendations). Include pointer(s) to machine-readable artifacts in the revised memo.
feedback_summary: Submission is largely correct and passed artifact-contract checks; computed scores, classifications, sensitivity analysis, and remediation combinations are consistent with the memo and normalized actual_outputs. The grader could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable), so claims in the memo about visible cell formulas and row-level citations remain unverified. The chosen scoring equation was documented but flagged 'needs_review' causing a small semantic deduction. Two records (R3, R4) show reconciliation variances and were correctly flagged as needing data review.

extra_model_fields.notes: Semantics (computed values and classifications) are consistent across the memo and ActualOutputs. The primary outstanding verification items are workbook formula exposure and explicit canonical scoring equation documentation.
recommended_actions: Manually open attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in a spreadsheet client to confirm formula visibility, row-level citations, and that no external links exist.

Provide an exported machine-readable representation of workbook formulas (CSV of formulas or a formula export) or a reproducible script that computes the same intermediate fields so automated graders can independently verify cell-level calculations.
If the scoring equation remains ambiguous in the task spec, explicitly state the canonical production equation and driver weights in both the memo and a machine-readable artifact.

verifier_issues.description: The artifact preview for attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] reports parse_status=openpyxl_unavailable with error 'No module named "openpyxl"'. As a result, the model grader relied on portfolio_memo.md and ActualOutputs for semantic checks rather than directly inspecting the workbook cells and formulas.
verifier_issues.title: Workbook could not be parsed by grader

verifier_issues.description: The portfolio memo asserts that the workbook includes row-level citations and formula lineage, but because the XLSX could not be opened in the grader environment this claim is unsupported by direct artifact inspection and requires manual verification of the workbook file.
verifier_issues.title: Unverified claims in memo about workbook formula exposure and row-level citations

verifier_issues.description: The memo documents the scoring equation used and marks it 'needs_review'. The grader applied a small deduction for choosing an interpretation when the canonical equation was unspecified.
verifier_issues.title: Scoring methodology selection and minor semantic penalty documented
